mechanism_narrative: Extremely low federal funds rates, as observed at 0.11%, indicate an aggressively accommodative monetary policy stance which significantly reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, thereby producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS value is near zero lower bound
failure_condition: If the average S&P 500 20-day return is negative during periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% over any 5-year rolling window, or if the Rank IC is consistently below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 7.5% signals a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, translating into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.5% during a declared post-recession recovery phase.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust consumer demand and investment, signaling broad economic strength that drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND HOUST growth > 2% MoM
failure_condition: If periods of concurrent strong growth (RSAFS > 0.5% MoM AND HOUST > 2% MoM) do not lead to statistically significant positive 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, a continued decline in INDPRO indicates worsening manufacturing conditions and reduced corporate output, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the short term (5 trading days).
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines during a confirmed industrial production contraction regime are consistently followed by positive S&P 500 5-day returns, or if the Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation, indicated by CPIAUCSL's 3-month annualized change exceeding 3%, suggests potential monetary tightening by the central bank, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: If periods of CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeding 3% are not followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns on average, or if the average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and S&P 500 20-day returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad economic downturn and weakening consumer demand, which severely impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 0% AND RSAFS growth < 0% MoM
failure_condition: If periods where both GDPC1 and RSAFS are contracting simultaneously (negative QoQ and MoM respectively) are followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagnant labor market, high unemployment (UNRATE > 7.0%) combined with near-zero or negative payroll growth (PAYEMS < 0.1% MoM) signals persistent weakness in the job market, dampening consumer sentiment and leading to short-term negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day returns are positive on average when UNRATE is above 7.0% and PAYEMS growth is minimal or negative, or if the Rank IC is below 0.03.
